identity.name: Live Session
identity.definition: A Live Session is a time-bounded, real-time delivery instance of a Nearpod Lesson where a teacher controls the pace and students participate synchronously using a unique join code.

representations.heading: Card layout
representations.description: How a Live Session appears in session dashboards and recent activity

attributes.name: Join Code
attributes.description: Unique alphanumeric code students use to join the session.

attributes.name: Session Status
attributes.description: Current state: scheduled, active, paused, ended.

attributes.name: Participant Count
attributes.description: Number of students currently connected or who participated.

representations.heading: List layout
representations.description: How a Live Session appears in session history and management tables

attributes.name: Class
attributes.description: The class participating in this session.

representations.heading: Detail page layout
representations.description: Full session view during delivery or post-session review

attributes.name: Session ID
attributes.description: Unique system identifier for the session.

attributes.name: Session Status
attributes.description: Lifecycle state: scheduled, active, paused, ended.

attributes.name: End Time
attributes.description: When the session ended. Null while active.

attributes.name: Participation Rate
attributes.description: Percentage of enrolled students who joined.

attributes.name: Session Link
attributes.description: Shareable URL for joining the session.

stories.title: Launch Live Session
stories.benefit: my students can join with a code and I can pace the lesson in real time.
stories.whenClause: I launch a lesson as a live session,
stories.thenClause: a unique join code is generated, the session status becomes active, and I can begin advancing slides.

stories.title: Monitor Participation
stories.benefit: I can see who has joined and ensure all students are engaged before advancing.
stories.whenClause: I view the session dashboard during delivery,
stories.thenClause: I see the participant count, participation rate, and real-time response streaming for each activity.

stories.title: Join Live Session
stories.benefit: I can participate in the lesson and respond to activities as the teacher presents them.
stories.whenClause: I enter the join code on my device,
stories.thenClause: I am connected to the session and see the current slide synchronized with the teacher's pace.

businessRules.title: Join Code Uniqueness
businessRules.description: Each Live Session receives a system-generated, unique alphanumeric join code. Codes are 5 characters and case-insensitive.

businessRules.title: Single Active Session
businessRules.description: A teacher can only have one active Live Session at a time. Launching a new session requires ending the current one.

businessRules.title: Lesson Requirement
businessRules.description: A Live Session must reference a published Lesson. Draft or archived Lessons cannot be launched.

businessRules.title: Participant Tracking
businessRules.description: Participant Count updates in real time as students join. Students who disconnect and rejoin are counted once.

businessRules.title: Pause Behavior
businessRules.description: Pausing a session freezes all student screens. Students cannot submit responses while paused. The teacher must resume to continue.

businessRules.title: End Session Finality
businessRules.description: Ending a session is irreversible. All pending responses are finalized and a Report is automatically generated.

businessRules.title: Session Duration
businessRules.description: Duration is calculated as the elapsed time from Start Time to End Time, excluding paused intervals.

businessRules.title: Late Join
businessRules.description: Students who join after the session has started see the current slide. They cannot see or respond to previously passed activities.

businessRules.title: Session Expiration
businessRules.description: Active sessions automatically end after 4 hours of inactivity (no slide advances or responses).

businessRules.title: Participation Rate Calculation
businessRules.description: Participation Rate is calculated as (Participant Count / enrolled students in Class) * 100.

businessRules.title: Report Generation
businessRules.description: A Report is automatically generated when a session ends. It includes per-activity breakdowns, participation data, and aggregate scores.

states.name: Scheduled
states.description: Session is configured but not yet started
states.triggers: Teacher schedules a future session launch

states.name: Active
states.description: Session is live, students can join and participate

states.name: Paused
states.description: Session is temporarily frozen, no responses accepted

states.name: Ended
states.description: Session is complete, report is generated

relationships.mechanics: A Live Session is created from a published Lesson. The Session inherits the Lesson's slide sequence and activities.
relationships.cardinality: Many-to-one. Multiple Live Sessions can reference the same Lesson; each Session references exactly one Lesson.
relationships.dependencies: The source Lesson must exist and be in 'published' state to launch a session.

relationships.mechanics: Students join a Live Session using the join code. Their participation and responses are tracked throughout the session.
relationships.cardinality: One-to-many. A Live Session has many student participants; a Student can participate in many Sessions over time.
relationships.sorts: By join time (default), by name, by response count.
relationships.filters: By participation status (joined/not-joined), by response completion.
relationships.dependencies: Student responses are finalized when the session ends. If a Student is removed from the class, their session data persists.

relationships.mechanics: A Report is auto-generated when a Live Session ends. It aggregates all participant responses and activity scores.
relationships.cardinality: One-to-one. Each Live Session produces exactly one Report.
relationships.dependencies: The Report is generated only after the session ends. It persists independently of the session state.

nestedObjects.name: Activity
nestedObjects.description: Interactive activities delivered during the session (inherited from Lesson)

nestedObjects.name: Student
nestedObjects.description: Students participating in the live session

nestedObjects.name: Score
nestedObjects.description: Scores generated from student responses during the session

allAttributes.name: Session ID
allAttributes.description: Unique system identifier for the session.

allAttributes.name: Join Code
allAttributes.description: 5-character alphanumeric code students use to join. Case-insensitive, unique per active session.

allAttributes.name: Session Status
allAttributes.description: Current lifecycle state. Values: scheduled, active, paused, ended.

allAttributes.name: Start Time
allAttributes.description: When the session was launched or scheduled to start.

allAttributes.name: End Time
allAttributes.description: When the session ended. Null while active or paused.

allAttributes.name: Duration
allAttributes.description: Elapsed time in minutes, excluding paused intervals.

allAttributes.name: Participant Count
allAttributes.description: Number of unique students who joined the session.

allAttributes.name: Lesson Reference
allAttributes.description: The lesson being delivered in this session.

allAttributes.name: Teacher
allAttributes.description: The teacher conducting the live session.

allAttributes.name: Class
allAttributes.description: The class participating in this session.

allAttributes.name: Session Link
allAttributes.description: Shareable URL for joining the session.

allAttributes.name: Participation Rate
allAttributes.description: Percentage of enrolled class students who joined the session.

structure.evidence: A Live Session has distinct attributes: session ID, join code, status, start/end times, participant count, participation rate, and references to lesson, teacher, and class. It supports a real-time dashboard view.

instances.evidence: Session ABCD1 (active, 24 students, 'Intro to Ecosystems', Ms. Rodriguez), Session XYZ42 (ended, 28 students, 'Fractions', Mr. Chen). A school runs dozens of sessions daily.

purpose.evidence: Live Sessions are the primary delivery mechanism for Nearpod's real-time instruction. Teachers launch them, students join them, and reports are generated from them. They are the bridge between static content and active learning.

sipValidation.verdict: Domain object representing the real-time delivery context. The runtime counterpart to the static Lesson.

objectViews.description: The session launch card with join code, class, and lesson reference. Teachers share the code with students and launch the session from here.
The full session dashboard — participant count, pacing controls, and real-time engagement metrics. Teachers advance slides, launch activities, and manage the session live.
Completed sessions listed with participation rates, duration, and timing. Teachers review session outcomes and access detailed reports.
